2)  interacting (epistasis)
d. Lethal alleles
e. Pleiotropy
f. Variable Penetrance/Expressivity
g. Linkage
h.  Cytoplasmic inheritance
i. Transposable elements

Summary
Genes on the same chromosome travel together as a unit
The most common offspring will be the parental chromosomes, because crossing over is relatively rare. The additional rare classes are recombinants

Historical Aside…….
Chromosomal Theory of Inheritance
Walter Sutton and Theodor Boveri (1902).
Calvin Bridges 1916:
Nondisjunction as proof of the Chromosomal Theory of Inheritance
Drosophila Life Cycle

Genetic Nomenclature in Drosophila genetics
“wild-type” is the most common allele in nature often represented as “+”
If not specified, + is dominant
With mutants, the lower case in mutant name means it is recessive, a Capital letter signifies the mutant is dominant.
If trait is not specified, can assume its wild-type

Note that Recombinant class is far lower than parentals

Recombination
The genetic distance between genes can be determined by measuring the percentage of recombinants.
Sturtevant and Morgan proposed that genes that are close together will have fewer opportunities for recombination, than those that are far away.
The distance between genes can defined as the % recombinants.

Determination of Map distance
(Test cross)
pr+ pr; vg+ vg   X   	pr pr; vg vg
Observed		Number	Class
pr+ pr; vg+ vg		1339   		Parental
pr+ pr; vg  vg		151		Crossover
pr   pr; vg+ vg		154		Crossover
pr  pr;  vg   vg		1195		Parental
Total offspring       2839
Total Recombinants:  151+154=305
% Recombinants of total : (305/2839) x 100
0.107        x 100  =   10.7%
Map distance between pr and vg= 10.7 Map units
10.7 centiMorgans

Example Problem
Map units sdrawkcab
The R/r and S/s genes are linked and 10 map units apart. In the cross Rs/rS by  rs/rs what fraction of the progeny will be RS/rs?

Important Terms, Concepts
Meiosis generates diversity
Chromosomes contain many genes in a “linkage group”
Linked genes can recombine
Recombination between homologous chromosomes occurs 	during Prophase I of Meiosis
Chiasma
Drosophila genetics involved the introduction of a new genetic
nomenclature
Drosophila genetics proved the Chromosomal Theory of
Inheritance
2 linked genes, both heterozygous can be in cis or trans
Recombination frequency can be used to measure distances
between linked genes
Distances between linked genes is measured in % recombinants
or Centimorgans
